name: Zakov
description:
Zakov

Key Demographics: Humans Capital City: Zakov (the city has no other name)

*{Entanglements

description[leftmargin=*, style=nextline] [To the West:] Thepyrgos -- The chain-towers and the Synod's seal police the straits; the Reaping's crystal hums under the keel. A Zakov corsair is a pirate; a Thepyrgos privateer is a legal pirate. [To the North:] Valewood (Thin Coast) -- Star-road shards and mist drift down the coast; the Gray Lady's agents watch the woods. The Reaping's influence does not cross the treeline. [To the South:] Theona -- The Green Host does not ride the waves, but the Ninth Taboo is whispered in the coves. The Zakov smugglers know which coves avoid the Host's riders. [To the East:] Aelinnel -- The gnomish tide-traders bring copper and counting-songs; the Reaping does not trust the fae. The gnomish courts have a standing offer on Astroegro artifacts. description

*{Quick Reference Tagline: The tide keeps a better tally than any scribe. The reef remembers every secret.

quote Salt Prince (Elite): "The tide here keeps better tallies than any scribe, and the reefs remember every secret whispered above their depths. Power flows like the currents -- those who understand them rule the waves. The Astroegro learned that. They are at the bottom now. The Kahfagians think they are different. They are not. The sea does not care about flags. It only cares about weight." quote

quote Dock-Rat (Commoner): "The sewers and the sea both carry what folks want to forget, but they also remember everything. Best keep your business above water and your enemies guessing which dock you'll use next. The Salt Prince's crown floats, the Reaping grows in the dark, and the tide pulls for anyone with a sharp knife and a sharper tongue. Lately, the Gray Lady has been asking questions. She's not from here. She's from somewhere colder. And she's not the only one." quote

Genre / Mood: Lovecraftian dread meets pirate swagger; eldritch tide-horror, maritime noir, pressure as a living force.

Starting Location: A smuggler's gate built into the seawall, its iron mouth crusted with barnacles and secrets. The gatekeeper is a one-eyed woman who accepts payment in stories. Behind her, the Dregs Quarter stretches on stilts into the harbor, and the Salt Prince's flagship, the Red Wake, rides at anchor off the Iron Pier. "The levy rises tonight," she says, spitting into the water. "Every ship pays, or every ship burns. You look like the burning type. Good. The Prince needs a reminder that the tide turns for everyone. And keep your eyes off the water at low tide. The Reaping doesn't like being watched. The Gray Lady has been asking about the Reaping. She's not the only one. The Oligarchs want the same thing. They're not allies. They're competition. You'd do well to remember which side you're on."

[colback=black!3,colframe=blue!60!black,title={Theme & Atmosphere,breakable] Zakov is the pirate-city of the Dolmis coast, built on the drowned bones of an Astroegro port city that sank beneath the waves when that ancient empire fell. The old stone ruins --- shattered columns, flooded plazas, sealed vaults --- lie only a few fathoms down, and at low tide you can see their roofs breaking the surface like the spines of dead leviathans. The city's streets are built on the rubble, its piers driven into the wreckage, and its deepest cellars open into sunken chambers where the water is warm and still. The Astroegro are gone, but their secrets remain: crystalline shards that hum with a terrible patience, sealed doors that weep brine, and the slow, creeping corruption that the locals call the Reaping.

Zakov's streets are piers, its courts are taverns, and its rulers are whoever the Syndicates fear most this season. The Salt Prince claims the throne of driftwood and extortion, but the real power ebbs and flows with the tide --- and the tide remembers everything. Here, every manifest is a lie, every harbor a doorstep to a dozen conspiracies, and every fog-light a lure or a warning. The city breathes with the sea: at low tide, secrets exposed; at flood, the drowned walk the Sunken Quarter and knock on doors that should be locked. Zakov is not a place you visit. It is a place you survive --- or rule.

Above the water, three powers circle. Naval Intelligence watches from the Ember, a Kahfagian cutter permanently anchored in the smuggler's gate, waiting for the Syndicates to make a mistake. Patron Alaric funds the Oligarchs' artifact hunts from a counting-house in the Crimson Docks, a man whose glass eye records everything it sees. And the Salt-Bitten, a cult that worships the Reaping's hunger, grows in the sewers and the sunken cellars, fed by the Ordo Artificum's scholars and the Oligarchs' greed.

Beneath it all, the Reaping. Centuries ago, the first Salt Prince --- then a common adventurer --- dove into a submerged Astroegro vault off the coast. What he found there no one knows for certain. Some say it was a living crystal, a fragment of a god that fell into the sea. Others whisper it is the father of Khemesh, the first hunger. The oldest tales call it simply the Reaping: a hunger that does not kill, but harvests. The Reaping seeps into Zakov like groundwater, corrupting slowly, tainting every soul who stays too long. Dreams curdle into obsession. Generosity rots into expectation. The city's famous "everyone owes everyone" is not a culture; it is a symptom. The Salt Prince keeps the Reaping's source sealed beneath his palace, but the seal is cracking, and the crystal grows.

What you notice first: The smell of brine and tar. The constant slap of water against stone. The way every tavern has a door that leads to a different dock --- and every dock has a name that changes with the tide. The faint, harmonic hum that comes from the deep, like a bell that never stops ringing. And the Gray Lady's mast, visible from every pier in the harbor, her steel hull dark against the salt-crusted wood.

The one rule that kills newcomers: Never take a coin that tastes of brine. The Serpent's Mark is not a blessing. It is a weight that drowns. And never look into a still pool at low tide. The Reaping looks back. The Gray Lady's agents look too.

*{Regional Mood: Salt, Serpent, and the Crystalline Deep

Here, the sea is not a border --- it is a landlord. Zakov rose from the wreckage of a sunken Astroegro port, and every stone is a salvage claim. The Salt Prince rules from a throne of driftwood and extortion, but the real lords are the Syndicate factors who own the docks, the eel-cults that whisper from the sewers (descended from Astroegro cultists who worshipped the deep), and the fae courts that launder their influence through tide-bargains. Ships arrive with goods no customs post will name, and depart with cargoes no manifest will show. The city's law is the Tide Timer : low water for killing, flood water for trading, slack for negotiation. Break the rhythm, and the city will break you.

Above the tide, a quieter war is fought. The Kahfagian Admiralty has known about Zakov's secrets for decades, and the Ordo Artificum, its secretive arcane division, has confirmed the Reaping's nature as a failed Astroegro containment system. Naval Intelligence, operating through deniable agents and cutouts, moves to contain the threat. The Oligarchs, funded by Patron Alaric, hunt the Astroegro artifacts that could control the Reaping --- or weaponize it. They fund the Syndicates, the Salt-Bitten, and even Naval Intelligence itself, playing every side to ensure they profit from the chaos. The agents of the Gray Lady and the Oligarchs' factors watch each other across the tavern tables of Zakov, trading secrets and bullets.

The Reaping is not a myth. It is a presence. It manifests as a crystalline growth that spreads through the submerged ruins, leaching color from stone and warmth from water. Those who stay in Zakov too long begin to dream of drowning; their shadows grow sharp and angular; their bargains feel heavier. The Reaping does not demand worship. It demands attention. And it has been waiting for centuries. The Ordo Artificum's scholars believe they can control it. The Salt-Bitten believe they can feed it. They are both wrong.

*{The Reaping --- A Campaign Timer

The Reaping is not a monster to be fought. It is a pressure, a corruption, a slow unmaking of the soul. Track the party's entanglement with it using a Reaping's Corrosion timer [8]. Tick the timer when: itemize A character accepts a bargain from the Salt Prince or a Syndicate factor. A character uses a Serpent's Mark (see Diamonds). A character fails a Resolve test while in Zakov (drowning dreams, crystalline whispers). The party lingers in the Sunken Quarter or the Serpent's Spine for more than a scene. The party handles an Astroegro artifact without protective measures. The party accepts aid from Naval Intelligence, the Oligarchs, or the Salt-Bitten. itemize At 4 segments: the character gains the Crystalline Whisper condition (you hear the Reaping's voice in quiet moments; --1 die to resist fear). Their shadow begins to shimmer faintly. At 8 segments: the character becomes Harvested. Their shadow detaches and walks into the sea, and they owe the Reaping a name (a true name of someone they love, or their own). They may still act, but they are marked. The GM may spend 2 SB to have a Collector --- a crystalline spirit in the shape of a drowned sailor --- appear and demand payment. To clear the timer, the party must: itemize Offer the Reaping something it values more than a soul: a memory of a crime no one witnessed, a true name from the Astroegro era (found in the sunken vaults), or a shard of its own prison (a heist into the Salt Prince's vault). Perform a deep-dive ritual at the Serpent's Spine, cutting a crystalline growth from the reef and replacing it with a lock of hair from a willing volunteer. Deliver an Astroegro artifact to the Ordo Artificum for "containment" --- though this rarely ends well for the artifact. itemize

*{Geographic Integration

Zakov sits at the cultural and maritime crossroads of the eastern Dolmis. To the north, the Valewood's mist-shrouded coasts send driftwood and nightmares down the current; the Reaping's crystalline hum is said to be an echo of the forest's deepest hungers. To the south, the Theona isles regard Zakov with wary tolerance --- the Ninth Taboo keeps the Green Host at bay, but Zakov's smugglers know which coves avoid the Host's riders. Across the Dolmis to the east, the gnomish Aelinnel of Aevrossa trade fine copper and stranger goods through Zakov's black market, their counting-songs an eerie counterpoint to the Reaping's harmonic pulse. The halfling Aelaerem of Amedell keep a wary distance, their red-thread wards flickering when the crystal grows strong.

The city's lifeblood is the narrow sea-lanes between the Dolmis and the Amaranthine. Thepyrgos controls the straits with its chain-towers and bell-warning systems, while Kahfagian patrols enforce tariffs on the open water. Zakov's pirates and smugglers must bribe, bluff, or blast past both to reach the rich southern waters. The Linns of the northern fjords sometimes portage their longships south from the Aberderrin into the Dolmis, trading amber and walrus ivory for Zakov's more questionable cargoes --- and leaving behind storm-oaths that the local Syndicates are careful not to break. The Kahfagian presence is the newest and most dangerous: Naval Intelligence's Ember is a permanent fixture, and the Oligarchs' factors are everywhere. The Syndicates have learned to tread carefully around the Gray Lady and her agents.

*{Prominent NPCs of Zakov

longtable{|p{0.18|p{0.25|p{0.3|p{0.17|

Name & Role/Title & Motivation & Rumoured Quirk

Salt Prince Lev & Nominal ruler of Zakov; keeper of the Reaping's seal & Keep the syndicates from tearing the city apart & His crown is a circlet of driftwood; he has never taken it off. Beneath it, his scalp is cracked like dry earth. Something crystalline moves in the cracks. He meets with Commodore Voss once a month. He always looks more tired afterward.

Pirate Queen Mila & Rules the Shallows with charm and cutlass & Carve out a kingdom free of the Salt Prince's levies & Her cutlass was her grandmother's; the grandmother was also a queen. The grandmother was the first to see the Reaping and refuse it. The refusal cost her her shadow. She does not know her grandmother was a Kahfagian agent. She does not know she is one too.

Commodore Voss & Commander, Kahfagian Naval Intelligence (Zakov station) & Contain the Reaping; recover Astroegro artifacts; keep the Syndicates in check & Her monocle is a polished Astroegro crystal shard. She sees the Reaping's corruption on anyone nearby. She has never taken it off. She sleeps with it on. She has not slept in three years.

Patron Alaric & Oligarch factor; head of the Zakov artifact trade & Acquire Astroegro artifacts; control the black market; feed the Ordo Artificum's research & His left eye is a functional Astroegro recording device. Everything he sees is archived. He has sold copies of his recordings to the Syndicates, to Naval Intelligence, and to the Salt-Bitten. He knows where the Reaping's source is. He is not telling.

Kraken's Tongue & Tide-oracle who speaks in riddles & Survive another season without being harvested & She lives in a bell tower that only exists at high tide; the bell does not ring. The bell is a fragment of the Reaping's prison. She tends it with her own blood. Her blood is crystalline. It glows in the dark. She has spoken to Commodore Voss once. She refused to tell her what she saw.

Scribe Marlo & Ordo Artificum scholar & Escape the Oligarchs' influence; expose the Ordo's true purpose & His hands are stained with crystal dust from handling artifacts. The dust has begun to grow. He has begun to dream in crystalline. He is afraid of what he will become. He knows the Ordo Artificum is not studying the artifacts. They are feeding them.

The Salt-Bitten Queen & Leader of the Reaping cult & Awaken the Reaping; become its harvesters & She has no eyes --- only crystalline sockets. She can see the Reaping's future. She is leading her followers into the Sunken Quarter to build a crystalline cathedral. She has made a deal with Patron Alaric. He will not speak of it.

Dock-Master Olena & Syndicate factor who "loses" ships & Own the waterfront, one weight at a time & Her tally has a secret page that is a map of debtors' bones. The bones are crystalline. They move. She has sold information to Commodore Voss three times. She has sold information to Patron Alaric twice. She has not decided which side she is on.

The Ember's Bosun & Adjutant agent, Kahfagian Naval Intelligence & Gather intelligence; maintain the Admiralty's presence & Her left arm is prosthetic, made from salvaged Astroegro alloy. It never tarnishes. It hums at low tide. She has been in Zakov for twelve years. She does not remember what she was before. She does not want to.

longtable

*{Names of Zakov

Inspired by the Black Sea and Odessa: Slavic, Turkic, and Greek inflections, with nicknames that stick like barnacles. Some families carry Astroegro loanwords as secret names, whispered only at the lowest tide.

*{(Harbor/Bolt-hole/Black Market) Salt-cracked wharf where tide brings back what law forgets; Bone-yard beach with wrecks and corpses in kelp; Smuggler's Gate behind the Fish Market; the Gray Lady's berth in the hidden cove; the Oligarch's auction barge in the Shallows.
